description: Legacy-terminology policy for current authoritative / customer-facing files. Enforced by tests/test_taxonomy_terminology.py. Forbidden terms must not appear in the scanned files except on lines explicitly marked as legacy/alias/drift context (legacy_marker_tokens) or via an explicit per-file exception below. This file is a policy declaration, NOT a second product catalog: board/SKU truth stays in config/hardware-catalog.json, lifecycle truth in config/product-catalog.json, the build matrix in config/webflash-builds.json, and the config-string contract in config/webflash-compatibility.json.

forbidden_terms.label: VentIQ Base/Pro variant
forbidden_terms.replacement: Sense360 VentIQ (S360-211) with connector attachments named explicitly
forbidden_terms.reason: There is no VentIQ Base/Pro product axis. S360-211 is one board (SGP41 on board; connectors for IR surface temperature and SPS30). Optional connector attachments are not product variants.

forbidden_terms.label: AirIQ Base/Pro variant
forbidden_terms.replacement: Sense360 AirIQ (S360-210) with connector attachments named explicitly
forbidden_terms.reason: There is no AirIQ Base/Pro product axis. S360-210 is one board (SCD41, SGP41, MICS-4514+STM8 on board; connectors for SPS30 and SFA40).

forbidden_terms.label: Bathroom Base/Pro legacy product name
forbidden_terms.reason: Bathroom Pro is the retired old name of the VentIQ board; a Base/Pro axis must not be reintroduced.

forbidden_terms.label: BathroomAirIQ legacy token
forbidden_terms.reason: Retired config-string token; read-time alias only.

forbidden_terms.label: BMP390 sensor-identity drift
forbidden_terms.replacement: BMP581 (RoomIQ S360-200 pressure sensor); AirIQ/VentIQ have no catalog pressure part
forbidden_terms.reason: config/hardware-catalog.json lists BMP581 on RoomIQ and no pressure sensor on AirIQ/VentIQ. BMP390 exists only as tracked compiled-firmware drift; current docs may reference it solely in explicitly marked drift/legacy context.

forbidden_terms.label: VEML7700 sensor-identity drift
forbidden_terms.reason: config/hardware-catalog.json lists LTR-303ALS. VEML7700 is the tracked compiled-firmware drift (ENTITY-RECONCILE-200-ALS-001); current docs may reference it solely in explicitly marked drift/legacy context.

forbidden_terms.label: HLK-PM01 part-identity drift
forbidden_terms.reason: The catalog part is HLK-5M05. The HLK-PM01/HLK-10M05 disagreement is a tracked hardware-evidence record, not a current-doc fact.

forbidden_terms.label: Model/Variant sensor table
forbidden_terms.replacement: single-SKU sensor table with connector attachments listed separately
forbidden_terms.reason: The product taxonomy is flat (one SKU per product); Variant-axis sensor tables must not appear in current docs.

exceptions.reason: Canonical SKU table 'Old name' column (category C: legacy-name reference).
Canonical SKU table 'Old name' column (category C: legacy-name reference).
Section 3 deprecated/forbidden-token mapping table (category C: required alias mapping).
Section 3 deprecated/forbidden-token mapping table (category C: required alias mapping).

classified_legacy_locations.description: Paths where legacy terminology legitimately remains, with classification. A=current-correct, B=historical-archive-only, C=compatibility-alias-required, F=tracked code/config drift. These paths are deliberately NOT scanned by the forbidden-term test.

paths.reason: Legacy alias filenames (airiq_bathroom_*.yaml, comfort_*.yaml, presence_*.yaml, ventiq.yaml, bathroom.yaml) are customer-pinned include paths and must keep resolving. Never rename.
Legacy functional include paths retained as aliases of the board packages; customer-pinned.
Compiles BMP390 with an in-file FIRMWARE/CATALOG DRIFT annotation. Firmware behaviour change is out of audit scope; tracked by the roadmap drift records.
Compiles BMP390 with an in-file FIRMWARE/SCHEMATIC drift annotation; module_variant tokens retained for compatibility.
Compiles the VEML7700 driver; catalog/BOM part is LTR-303ALS. Disposition is owner-only (ENTITY-RECONCILE-200-ALS-001).

Long-form legacy hardware reference (Mini/Wall/Desk lines, Comfort/Presence split boards, Bathroom Base/Pro tables). Demoted to legacy-reference status by PRODUCT-TAXONOMY-AUDIT-001; retained as historical hardware-level detail.
Mini board sections retained as historical CI records, explicitly marked retired (PRODUCT-DEP-MINI-001).
Drift-tracking record; names BMP390/VEML7700/SFA40 deliberately as tracked reconciliation items.
Framework architecture docs document the drift correctly (no Base/Pro axis; BMP390/VEML7700 flagged as drift).
Hardware evidence records quote stale part identities (HLK-PM01 or similar, BMP390 footprints) verbatim as evidence. Never rewrite evidence.
Mini/Wall tombstone entries record retirement (removal_reason); they are current lifecycle truth, not stale text.
Documents the compiled (drifted) parts deliberately, with catalog conflicts annotated.
Mini/Wall/Basic/Advanced name mappings serve tag-pinned legacy releases; read-time compatibility.
Tests pin legacy tokens and aliases on purpose (forbidden-token guards, alias fixtures).
basic/advanced profile filenames are behaviour-profile names, not product tiers; customer-pinned include paths.
